message:
fix(codegen): Set current_function_name when generating closure bodies

When generating closure body statements, the `current_function_name` was
not updated to the closure's name. This caused `isVarUnused()` to look up
the wrong function's usage data (the outer function instead of the closure),
incorrectly detecting local variables as "unused".

The fix adds save/restore for `current_function_name` in both closure
generation paths:
- genStandardClosure
- genNestedFunctionWithOuterCapture

Example of the bug:
```python
def grade(score, breakpoints=[60, 70, 80, 90], grades='FDCBA'):
    i = self.module.bisect(breakpoints, score)  # i detected as "unused"
    return grades[i]  # but i is used here!
```

The assignment to `i` was wrapped in a decref block and discarded instead
of creating a local variable, causing "use of undeclared identifier 'i'".

Test impact:
- test_bisect now passes (was failing due to `i` undeclared)
- core1: 15/65 (23.1%) - up from 14/65 (21.5%)
